title: Frontend Frameworks Best Practices Quiz
description: Test your knowledge about the essential best practices for modern frontend development.

questions.question: What is the core idea behind Atomic Design?
options: Always using global state management.
Breaking down complex UI into small, reusable building blocks (atoms).
Writing all code in a single file.
Using only CSS animations.

questions.question: Why is immutability important in state management?
options: It makes the application slightly faster.
It ensures predictable rendering and helps avoid subtle bugs.
It's the only way to use state.
It's a requirement for all CSS frameworks.

questions.question: What is the primary benefit of lazy loading?
options: It makes the application look better.
It reduces the initial bundle size and speeds up the first load.
It automatically generates responsive layouts.
It's the only way to use images.

questions.question: Which of these is NOT a good practice for accessibility?
options: Using semantic HTML tags.
Ensuring all interactive elements are keyboard-accessible.
Only testing on high-resolution monitors.
Using ARIA attributes where needed.

questions.question: What is the goal of an integration test in frontend development?
options: To test a single function in isolation.
To ensure that multiple components or units of code work together correctly.
To verify the entire user flow from the server to the browser.
To check the CSS for responsiveness.
